---
title: "Build a PTI"
description: >
  Overview of the seven-step pipeline that takes raw country data through to
  a deployed Project Targeting Index (PTI) Shiny dashboard with `devPTIpack`.
---

A **Project Targeting Index (PTI)** is a composite map ranking sub-national administrative units against a set of weighted indicators — useful for guiding the spatial allocation of programmes and investments. `devPTIpack` is the toolkit that turns your country's shapes + indicator data into a deployed Shiny dashboard.

This page is the **journey map**. Each step is a focused tutorial; this overview tells you which one to read when, and how the pieces fit together. Pair it with [PTI Methodology](methodology.html) (the conceptual *why*) and [Data preparation reference](dataprep.html) (the canonical column-by-column *what*).

## What data does a PTI app need?

Two inputs are mandatory; one is optional:

| Input              | Format                                                            | Step that produces it                                            |
| ------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Boundary shapes    | Named list of `sf` tibbles, one per admin level.                  | [Step 1](build-pti-1-shapefiles.html)                            |
| Metadata workbook  | Multi-sheet `.xlsx` — indicator dictionary + per-admin values.    | [Step 3](build-pti-3-metadata.html)                              |
| HEX gridded data   | H3 res-6 polygons + indicator values. *Optional.*                 | [Step 4](build-pti-4-hex.html) (under development)               |

The full column contract for both lives in [Data preparation reference](dataprep.html).

## Reproducible quickstart — the bundled Rwanda app in < 5 lines

The fastest way to see a working PTI app is the Rwanda template that ships with the package:

```r
#| eval: false
install.packages("pak"); pak::pak("worldbank/devPTIpack")
library(devPTIpack)
create_new_pti("~/projects/rwa_pti")           # scaffold
source("~/projects/rwa_pti/00-master.R")       # Steps 1 + 3 (+ 5 once enabled)
shiny::runApp("~/projects/rwa_pti/app.R")      # local launch
```

Five Rwandan provinces, 30 districts, 3 synthetic indicators, two pillars — a complete dashboard in under five minutes. Once that runs, swap in your country's data and re-run the same pipeline.

## The journey

The pipeline is seven steps. Steps 1–5 produce files in `app-data/`; the deployed app consumes them.

| # | Step                                                  | What it does                                                                                | Required?     |
| - | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- |
| 0 | [Setup](build-pti-0-setup.html)                       | Install `devPTIpack`, scaffold the project folder via `create_new_pti()`.                   | Always        |
| 1 | [Shapefiles](build-pti-1-shapefiles.html)             | Load + validate administrative boundaries; save `app-data/shapes.rds`.                       | Always        |
| 2 | [Zonal stats](build-pti-2-zonal-stats.html)           | Extract polygon-level indicators from raster surfaces.                                       | Optional      |
| 3 | [Metadata Excel](build-pti-3-metadata.html)           | Build the indicator workbook; validate against shapes; save `app-data/metadata-user.xlsx`.    | Always        |
| 4 | [HEX data](build-pti-4-hex.html)                      | Pull H3 res-6 indicator values from the HEX API. *Order-flexible* — before or after Step 3.   | Optional      |
| 5 | [Compile & finalise](build-pti-5-compile.html)        | Merge metadata + re-validate + render PDF + zip GeoJSONs.                                    | Always        |
| 6 | [Deploy](build-pti-6-deploy.html)                     | Publish `app.R` + `landing-page.md` + `app-data/` to Posit Connect or shinyapps.io.           | When sharing  |

**Step 4 is order-flexible** — it produces an independent `app-data/metadata-hex.xlsx` that Step 5 merges with the user metadata. You can do it before or after Step 3. The other steps run sequentially.

## The `00-master.R` shortcut

Each scaffolded project ships [`00-master.R`](https://github.com/worldbank/devPTIpack/blob/main/inst/template_pti/00-master.R) — a pipeline orchestrator that calls `quarto::quarto_render()` on the step `.qmd` files top-to-bottom, populates `app-data/`, and (optionally) writes the rendered Quarto trail under `docs/`. After your first walkthrough, **`source("00-master.R")` is the everyday command** — it re-runs Steps 1, (3), 5 every time your raw inputs change. Comment / uncomment lines to skip optional steps (Step 2, 4) or pause work.

The website tutorials (this page and Steps 0–6) are how you *learn* the pipeline. `00-master.R` is how you *operate* it once you know it.

## Where to start

- **First time?** Begin with [Step 0 — Setup](build-pti-0-setup.html) and follow the steps top to bottom.
- **Already familiar with `devPTIpack`?** Jump straight to the step you're working on. Each tutorial has a "Next" pointer back into the chain.
- **Reference question** ("what columns does the metadata sheet need?") — go to [Data preparation reference](dataprep.html).
- **Method question** ("how is the PTI score computed?") — go to [PTI Methodology](methodology.html).
